Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant Andreas Angelopoulos Appearance At Deposition And To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREAS ANGELOPOULOS TRUSTEE OF THE ANDREAS ANGELOPOULOS AND POLLY ANGELOPOULOS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant Andreas Angelopoulos Appearance At Deposition And To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And Monetary Sanctions.

The court denies Defendant Andreas Angelopoulos's motion to set aside this court's order granting Plaintiff Thomas Karas's motion to compel Defendant's appearance at deposition, to deem RFAs admitted, and for monetary sanctions. Angelopoulos seeks relief under CCP 473 and contends he did not receive notice of the motion or hearing. Angelopoulos has not carried his burden of showing excusable neglect. A party in a designated case (including civil cases like this one) are required to use e-filing. Service may be made through the e-filing system. (Local Rule 2.11(Q).) Although Angelopoulos complains that his paralegal's email address was the only one used to effect service, this is the email address that was used in his prior e-filings, and appears to be his designated e-filing address for this case. The service he complains of was made through the e-filing system. Angelopoulos has not shown with affirmative evidence or analysis in his moving papers that this email address was incorrect under the Code of Civil Procedure, the Rules of Court, or the Local Rules applicable to electronic service of moving papers, and if in fact his paralegal's email address is the designated e-filing address for this case, then service was proper and Angelopoulos's mistake was unreasonable. Nothing in this order precludes Angelopoulos from seeking new relief based on changed circumstances or new facts related to his health, but the parties should meet and confer first about the necessity for any such relief.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PLAINTIFFs THOMAS KARAS AS TRUSTEE OF THE THOMAS A. KARAS AND ELENI KARAS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 7/10/2014, ELENI KARAS's Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Thomas A. Karas's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions to which no opposition has been filed is GRANTED. Defendant Andreas Angelopoulos is ordered to appear at deposition in this matter no later than January 10, 2025, such deposition may be conducted remotely and shall not exceed four hours but may be spread across two sessions. Karas's conduct related to this discovery issue has been quite reasonable and Angelopoulos's conduct has been unreasonable. Given Angelopoulos's complete failure to respond to other written discovery, deposition by response to written interrogatories is not a realistic option in this case. The requests for admissions served August 28, 2024, are deemed admitted. Angelopoulos's failure to respond was neither reasonable nor justified.

Angelopoulos's response to the subject discovery was not substantially justified. Angelopoulos and his counsel shall pay a total of $3,200 to Plaintiff as sanctions.

Plaintiff is advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frog Set, SRog Set, RFP Set, RFA Set, deposition notice) addressed to a single party.

Proposed Order submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
